title: DSDS Criterion Definition
description: Shared definitions for testable success criteria and external standard references. Criteria are the test-definition layer of DSDS documentation: each pairs a stable identifier with an objectively verifiable statement, so tooling and agents can check implementations against the docs and report pass/fail per criterion. Used by guideline entries and accessibility blocks. Observed results belong in a guideline entry's `evidence`, not here.

reference.description: A citation of an external standard or requirement — a published rule this documentation addresses (WCAG, platform guidelines, vendor compliance frameworks).

url.description: URL to the external standard or requirement (e.g., 'https://www.w3.org/TR/WCAG22/#contrast-minimum').

label.description: A display label for the citation (e.g., '1.4.3 Contrast (Minimum)', 'WCAG 2.5.8 Target Size'). When omitted, tools MAY derive a label from the URL.

criterion.description: A testable success criterion: an objectively verifiable condition an implementation must meet. Criteria let documentation act as a test harness — a check runs against the `statement` and reports pass/fail under the criterion's `identifier`. Pair with `techniques` (how to pass), `failures` (what failing looks like), `references` (the external standards it aligns with), and `examples` (typically a passing/failing pair).

identifier.description: Stable identifier for this criterion, unique within the parent entity (e.g., 'touch-target-minimum', 'single-primary-action'). MUST be lowercase kebab-case. Test runs report pass/fail against this identifier, so it SHOULD NOT change once checks depend on it — renaming breaks result history across runs.

title.description: A short display name for the criterion (e.g., 'Touch Target Minimum'). Tools SHOULD render it in docs and test reports beside the identifier.

statement.description: The testable statement of what success looks like. MUST be objectively verifiable — by inspection, static analysis, or runtime measurement. Avoid vague language like 'feels right' or 'looks balanced' (e.g., 'Interactive elements present a hit area of at least 44×44 px').

techniques.description: Sufficient ways to satisfy this criterion — the passing patterns (e.g., 'Apply `min-height: 44px` to every interactive element', 'Use the `<Button>` component without overriding its default height').

failures.description: Known failure modes — conditions that count as failing this criterion (e.g., 'Setting `height` below 44px on an interactive element', 'Removing `aria-label` from an icon-only button').

references.description: External standards this criterion aligns with or derives from (e.g., a matching WCAG success criterion, a platform guideline).

examples.description: Examples that illustrate the criterion — typically a before/after pair: one passing and one failing implementation.

tags.description: Freeform keywords that relate criteria across guidelines and entities (e.g., 'a11y', 'contrast', 'touch-target').
